content: The linguistic definition of reflexion as both optical reflection and deliberate cognitive reflection can inform the design of better LLM self-reflection mechanisms: just as a physical mirror reflects light back to reveal an object's unseen features, LLM self-reflection should draw on inconsistent problem-solving perspectives (as documented in Self-Contrast research) to 'reflect back' the unrecognized flaws in the LLM's initial output. This frames multi-perspective LLM self-reflection not just as a cognitive trick, but as an engineered analog of the physical optical reflexion that gives the concept its name.
